title: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (Department of Accounting and General Services - Office of Elections)

communications.subject: Hawaii Uniform Information Practices Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (Department of Accounting and General Services - Office of Elections)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Hawaii Uniform Information Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Any and all memoranda of understanding (MOUs), agreements, contracts, or similar instruments between the state and the U.S. Department of Justice relating to the provision of voter registration data, voter files, or voter roll information, from January 20, 2025 to the present.

2. Any draft, unsigned, proposed, or rejected versions of such agreements.

3. Any amendments, addenda, or modifications to such agreements.

This request includes but is not limited to any document described or labeled as a "memorandum of understanding" or contract relating to voter data, voter list maintenance, or voter roll verification.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

communications.subject: RE: Hawaii Uniform Information Practices Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (Department of Accounting and General Services - Office of Elections)
communications.communication:

title: Virginia DOC FOIA

communications.subject: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Virginia DOC FOIA
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Virginia Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

I would like to request a document containing facility-month level data from January 2024 through December 2025 be provided on the below measures for every prison managed or owned by the Department of Corrections. 
•Overall staff budgeted positions 
•Overall staff filled positions 
•Custody staff budgeted positions 
•Custody staff filled positions 
•Average hours of overall staff overtime used
•Average hours of custody staff overtime used
•Average hours of overall staff sick leave used
•Average hours of custody staff sick leave used
•Overall population count 
•Total number of infractions 
•Total number of violent infractions (including the department’s definition of which infractions are defined as violent) 
•Total number in restricted housing 
•Total number of appeals (grievances) from incarcerated individuals

I am also requesting the name and email of the current, acting, and/or incoming wardens for each facility. This request is made for a scholarly or scientific purpose and not for commercial use. Thank you for your time.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Tom Nash, a citizen of Virginia, in coordination with Jessie Harney.

communications.subject: Re: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Virginia DOC FOIA
communications.communication:
Hi Mr. Nash,

Please call me to review your FOIA request.  I can be reached at (804) 963-2953.


Thank-you for contacting the Virginia Department of Corrections.

Sincerely,

Anne-Cabrié Forsythe
FOIA Officer
(804) 963-2953

communications.subject: Re: Virginia Freedom of Information Act Request: Virginia DOC FOIA
communications.communication:
Thank you for your request for records made in accordance with the Virginia Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) (§ 2.2-3700 et seq.) received on February 16, 2026. Please be advised that it is administratively impossible to provide the requested records or determine whether they are available within the five working days required by FOIA because of the scope of your request.

Therefore, we are invoking § 2.2-3704(B)(4) of the Code of Virginia to provide us seven additional working days to respond to your request.

Please be advised that in accordance with §2.2-3704(B) of the Code of Virginia, "[a] request for public records shall identify the requested records with reasonable specificity." Therefore, please identify the specific records that you are requesting in regard to total number of appeals (grievances) from incarcerated individuals.

Per my emails on February 18th and February 20th, please call me when you have a few minutes to review the FOIA request.  I can be reached at (804) 963-2953.

Thank-you for contacting the Virginia Department of Corrections.

Sincerely,


Anne-Cabrié Forsythe
FOIA Officer
(804) 963-2953

title: Linn Walsh Marin County Sheriff Civilian Oversight Commission  Communications

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Linn Walsh Marin County Sheriff Civilian Oversight Commission  Communications
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All emails of Linn Walsh to any member of the Marin County Sheriff Civilian Oversight Commission "COC" between January 1st, 2025 to December 24 2025.

All emails of Linn Walsh  to any January 1st, 2024 to December 24 2025 that includes the key terms "Civilian" "Oversight" "Commission" "3824"

All emails of Linn Walsh  to any January 1st, 2024 to December 24 2025 that includes the key terms that is related to the implementation of Ordinance 3824 and the COC.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Robbie Powelson

communications.subject: [External Message Added] County of Marin, CA public records request #25-1041
communications.communication:
County of Marin, CA ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #25-1041: Dear Requester, The County will conduct a search for the records you request, which the County anticipates will be completed by January 26, 2026. Please note that responsive documents pursuant to your request may not be produced based on the following: 1.     Confidential information protected by the attorney-client privilege or attorney work product doctrine. Government Code Section 7927.705 and Evidence Code Section 950 et seq. 2.     Records exempt because the public interest in withholding the records clearly outweighs the public interest in disclosure of the records. Government Code Section 7922.000. 3.     Information exempt from disclosure pursuant to the deliberative process privilege. See Government Code Section 7922.000 and case law setting forth the deliberative process privilege, including but not limited to, Times Mirror Company v. Superior Court (1991) 53 Cal.3d 1325 and Rogers v. Superior Court (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 469. 4.     Personnel, medical or similar files, the disclosure of which would constitute an unwarranted invasion of personal privacy. See Government Code Section 7927.700. Thank you. ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at County of Marin, CA.

Technical support: See our help page

title: Non-Law Enforcement ALPR Review

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Non-Law Enforcement ALPR Review
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents relating to usage of vehicle-based Automated License Plate Recognition (ALPR) technology in the City of San Francisco. This EXCLUDES all ALPR technology belonging to the San Francisco Police Department, and instead focuses on non-law enforcement ALPR systems and technology. For City of San Francisco / SFMTA ALPR technology, we are requesting:

1) NDAs, MSAs, SOWs, and contracts relating to the purchase and maintenance of ALPR systems owned/operated by the City of San Francisco / SFMTA
2) ALPR policies, procedures, guides, and training materials 
3)  The names of agencies and organizations with which the City of San Francisco / SFMTA shared ALPR data in 2025
4) All "records of access" required by Sec. 1798.90.52(a) for the periods June 1st, 2024 until the date of processing this request (An audit log of ALPR queries, time/date, identity/role, agency, and type of data accessed will suffice). If your agency has a retention period shorter than 1 year, please provide whatever data is available based on your retention period. 
5) Any MOU or data sharing agreement which allows San Francisco City / SFMTA ALPR records to be shared or queried by non-administrative city staff (e.g. federal agencies, SFPD, etc.).
6) Any search warrants, subpoenas, or court orders you have received for ALPR data in 2025. 

Previously, I submitted a FOIA to BAAQMD based on finding ALPR cameras on a vehicle with their logo on it. They referred me to the City of San Francisco, stating that you own the vehicle. The City of San Francisco stated that SFMTA had responsive documents. For more context, you may read my exchange with them and view a photo of the vehicle in question here: https://www.muckrock.com/foi/california-52/baaqmd-alpr-review-189686/

I appreciate your assistance. I'd be happy to chat and provide additional context to aid you in your search.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

communications.subject: [External Message Added] City and County of San Francisco public records request #26-1448
communications.communication:
City and County of San Francisco ************************************************************************ A message was sent to you regarding record request #26-1448:        Exemptions and Privileges The SFMTA has documents responsive to your request but is not producing some of the documents based on the following exemptions. The Public Records Act allows an agency to decline to disclose "records the disclosure of which is exempted or prohibited pursuant to federal or state law, including, but not limited to, provisions of the Evidence Code relating to privilege." See Gov. Code §7927.705, 7930.110 6254(k); see also, S.F. Admin. Code §67.21(k). California Evidence Code §954 protects from disclosure communications between attorneys and their clients. The California Supreme Court has confirmed that Government Code §7927.705, 7930.110 formerly §6254(k) authorizes a public entity to deny a member of the public access to a document by asserting the attorney-client privilege. See Roberts v. Palmdale, (1993) 5 Cal.4th 363. Similarly, California Code of Civil Procedure ("CCP") §2018 protects "any writing that reflects an attorney's impressions, conclusions, opinion, or legal research or theories." CCP §2018.030 protects the work product, not just attorney work product, generated by a public agency in anticipation of litigation. Fairley v. Superior Court of Los Angeles County, (1998) 66 Cal.App.4th 1414, 1422 fn. 5.  Some of the documents you have requested are exempt from disclosure under either the attorney-client privilege or the attorney work product privilege. Disclosure of the communications you have requested would impede the ability of the SFMTA to receive legal advice. Accordingly, we will not produce privileged attorney-client communications and attorney work product.   ************************************************************************ Questions about your request? Reply to this email or sign in to contact staff at City and County of San Francisco.

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Santa Barbara County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Santa Barbara County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Ransomware incidents (Connecticut State Police)

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: Ransomware incidents (Connecticut State Police)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Documents, including but not limited to emails, memos, and incident reports, mentioning or describing any reported or encountered incidents of "ransomware," which may be loosely defined as a type of malware that threatens to permanently encrypt and/or unless a ransom is paid.

I am a member of the news media and request classification as such. I have previously written about the government and its activities, with some reaching over 100,000 readers in outlets such as Gizmodo, MuckRock, Motherboard, Property of the People, Unicorn Riot, and The Outline, among others. As such, as I have a reasonable expectation of publication and my editorial and writing skills are well established. In addition, I discuss and comment on the files online and make them available through non-profits such as the library Internet Archive and the journalist non-profit MuckRock, disseminating them to a large audience. While my research is not limited to this, a great deal of it, including this, focuses on the activities and attitudes of the government itself. As such, it is not necessary for me to demonstrate the relevance of this particular subject in advance.

As my primary purpose is to inform about government activities by reporting on it and making the raw data available, I request that fees be waived.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be processed by a representative of the news media/press and made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Emma North-Best
An acknowledgement letter, stating the request is being processed.

communications.subject: RE: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: Ransomware incidents (Connecticut State Police)
communications.communication:

title: NSPM-7 docs (California Highway Patrol)

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: NSPM-7 docs (California Highway Patrol)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Hello, 

I am requesting records related to NSPM-7, antifa, domestic violent extremists, and other anti-terrorism designations. 

Please search for 
- Internal memos or memos from outside agencies about the designations above
- Bulletins, internally or shared from outside agencies about the designations above
- emails and their threads with the keyword "NSPM-7, "antifa," "domestic violent extremists," and/or "DVE"

- emails and their threads with the keywords: 
"Ending Political Violence Against ICE"
"Domestic Violent Extremists Pose Increased Threat of Violence to ICE Facilities and Personnel"
"DHS is Fighting Back Against Antifa Violence, Department ofHomeland Security"

- situational awareness reports, bulletins, and memos related to NSPM-7 and/or Antifa and/or Domestic Violent Extremists and/or DVE
- Powerpoint presentations

Please search: 

September 1, 2025 to today's date

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joey Scott

communications.subject: CPRA REQUEST :: R010947-102125
communications.communication:
The California Highway Patrol Public Records Section (PRS) received your request for records submitted on October 21, 2025 
For tracking purposes, your reference number is: R010947-102125 
You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you'll receive an email when your request has been completed. 
Thank you, 
California Highway Patrol

communications.subject: [Records Center] CPRA REQUEST :: R010947-102125
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

 
RE: CPRA REQUEST of October 22, 2025, Reference # R010947-102125. Dear Mr. Scott, The California Highway Patrol received your request for records on October 22, 2025. The Public Records Section (PRS) is determining whether you seek copies of disclosable public records in the Department’s possession.  Accordingly, the PRS is extending its 10-day response timeframe by an additional 14 days, as authorized by Government Code section 7922.535, subsection (c)(1). 

If you have any questions or need additional information, please respond to this email or contact the PRS at (916) 843-4030. 
Sincerely, Kortnee Graves 
California Highway Patrol 
Public Records Section

communications.subject: [Records Center] CPRA REQUEST :: R010947-102125
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

 RE: CPRA REQUEST of October 22, 2025, Reference # R010947-102125. Dear Joey Scott, The California Highway Patrol received your request for records on October 22, 2025. The Public Records Section (PRS) has determined the Department possesses records responsive to your request; however, the process of collecting, redacting, and reviewing this information will take approximately 30 days.  Once this process is complete or if additional time is needed, you will be notified. 

If you have any questions or need additional information, please respond to this email or contact the PRS at (916) 843-4030. 
Sincerely, Kortnee Graves 
California Highway Patrol 
Public Records Section

communications.subject: [Records Center] CPRA REQUEST :: R010947-102125
communications.communication:
--- Please respond above this line --- 

 RE: CPRA REQUEST of October 22, 2025, Reference # R010947-102125. Dear Joey Scott, On November 11, 2025, the PRS notified you that the Department possessed records responsive to your request. At this time the PRS estimates an additional 30 days are needed for the review and redaction process. Once this process is complete or if additional time is needed, you will be notified. If you have any questions or need additional information, please respond to this email or contact the PRS at (916) 843-4030. 
Sincerely, Kortnee Graves 
California Highway Patrol 
Public Records Section

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Indiana Secretary of State)

communications.subject: Indiana Access to Public Records Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (Indiana Secretary of State)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Indiana Access to Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

title: Records concerning Commissioner Trey Trainor's Romania Election Observation Visit (2025)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Records concerning Commissioner Trey Trainor's Romania Election Observation Visit (2025)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Records related to former FEC Commissioner James Edwin “Trey” Trainor III’s visit to Romania in May 2025, for the period January 1, 2025 through July 31, 2025. This request includes, but is not limited to:
a. Names, positions, and roles of all staff, participants, consultants, or accompanying personnel involved in the visit.
b. Memos, emails, or other documentation describing the circumstances under which each staff member was appointed or assigned to participate.
c. Travel authorizations, itineraries, per diem approvals, and any other logistical documentation associated with the visit.
d. Correspondence with the U.S. State Department, Romanian officials, or any other agencies regarding staff selection, participation, or planning of the visit.
e. Reports, summaries, evaluations, or notes documenting the meetings, observations, or outcomes of the visit, whether produced for internal or external purposes.
f. Internal briefings, instructions, or reports provided to staff before, during, or after the visit.
g. Whether the FEC endorsed, supported, or adopted any viewpoint, assessment, or conclusion regarding the May 2025 Romania election observation visit.
h. Any guidance, statements, or correspondence clarifying whether public remarks, interviews, or publications by Commissioner James Edwin “Trey” Trainor III or accompanying staff regarding the May 2025 Romania election observation visit are considered official FEC positions or attributable to the Commission.

For reference, the visit has been described on a Weekly Digest page as follows: "From May 2-5, Acting Chairman Trainor observed the Presidential elections in Romania."  https://www.fec.gov/updates/week-of-april-28-may-2-2025/

2. All records specifically concerning Dragoș Sprînceană, in connection with the above-referenced May 2025 Romania visit, election observation activities, or any other engagement, communication, or interaction with the Federal Election Commission from November 1, 2024 through October 31, 2025, including any records in which his name appears in variant spellings (e.g., “Dragos Sprinceana”, “Dragos Spranceana”).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

John Swoop

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request to the Federal Election Commission FOIA (2026-084)
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Swoop,

This email acknowledges receipt of your Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) request dated, February 8, 2026.

Specifically, you requested the following:

“1. Records related to former FEC Commissioner James Edwin “Trey” Trainor III’s visit to Romania in May 2025, for the period January 1, 2025 through July 31, 2025. This request includes, but is not limited to:
a. Names, positions, and roles of all staff, participants, consultants, or accompanying personnel involved in the visit.
b. Memos, emails, or other documentation describing the circumstances under which each staff member was appointed or assigned to participate.
c. Travel authorizations, itineraries, per diem approvals, and any other logistical documentation associated with the visit.
d. Correspondence with the U.S. State Department, Romanian officials, or any other agencies regarding staff selection, participation, or planning of the visit.
e. Reports, summaries, evaluations, or notes documenting the meetings, observations, or outcomes of the visit, whether produced for internal or external purposes.
f. Internal briefings, instructions, or reports provided to staff before, during, or after the visit.
g. Whether the FEC endorsed, supported, or adopted any viewpoint, assessment, or conclusion regarding the May 2025 Romania election observation visit.
h. Any guidance, statements, or correspondence clarifying whether public remarks, interviews, or publications by Commissioner James Edwin “Trey” Trainor III or accompanying staff regarding the May 2025 Romania election observation visit are considered official FEC positions or attributable to the Commission.
For reference, the visit has been described on a Weekly Digest page as follows: "From May 2-5, Acting Chairman Trainor observed the Presidential elections in Romania." https://www.fec.gov/updates/week-of-april-28-may-2-2025/
2. All records specifically concerning Dragoș Sprînceană, in connection with the above-referenced May 2025 Romania visit, election observation activities, or any other engagement, communication, or interaction with the Federal Election Commission from November 1, 2024 through October 31, 2025, including any records in which his name appears in variant spellings (e.g., “Dragos Sprinceana”, “Dragos Spranceana”).”

We received your request on February 9, 2026.

We will soon be initiating our search for records responsive to your request.  If we determine that you have requested a voluminous amount of records that require extensive searching and examination, we will contact you to discuss your willingness to modify your request.

Any questions regarding the status of this request should be directed to our office at foia@fec.gov or 202-694-1650.  Please cite the following unique tracking number in all future correspondence regarding this matter: FOIA Request 2026-084.

Thank you for contacting the Federal Election Commission.

Sincerely,

FEC FOIA Requester Service Center

files.title: ~WRD0002
files.source: Federal Election Commission, United States of America

communications.subject: Re: 	Your Freedom of Information Act Request to the Federal Election Commission FOIA [2026-084]
communications.communication:
VIA ELECTRONIC MAIL

204408-84881343@requests.muckrock.com<mailto:204408-84881343@requests.muckrock.com>

Re:       Your Freedom of Information Act Request to the Federal Election Commission FOIA [2026-084]

Dear Mr. Swoop,

This email is in response to the request you filed for information under the Freedom of Information Act (FOIA) dated and received by the Federal Election Commission's (FEC) FOIA Requester Service Center on February 8, 2026.  Specifically, you requested:
"1. Records related to former FEC Commissioner James Edwin "Trey" Trainor III's visit to Romania in May 2025, for the period January 1, 2025 through July 31, 2025. This request includes, but is not limited to:
a. Names, positions, and roles of all staff, participants, consultants, or accompanying personnel involved in the visit.
b. Memos, emails, or other documentation describing the circumstances under which each Staff member was appointed or assigned to participate.
c. Travel authorizations, itineraries, per diem approvals, and any other logistical documentation associated with the visit.
d. Correspondence with the U.S. State Department, Romanian officials, or any other agencies regarding staff selection, participation, or planning of the visit.
e. Reports, summaries, evaluations, or notes documenting the meetings, observations, or outcomes of the visit, whether produced for internal or external purposes.
f. Internal briefings, instructions, or reports provided to staff before, during, or after the visit.
g. Whether the FEC endorsed, supported, or adopted any viewpoint, assessment, or conclusion regarding the May 2025 Romania election observation visit.
h. Any guidance, statements, or correspondence clarifying whether public remarks, interviews, or publications by Commissioner James Edwin "Trey" Trainor III or accompanying staff regarding the May 2025 Romania election observation visit are considered official FEC positions or attributable to the Commission.
We have searched our records and located responsive documents. The Office of Government Ethics released details related to former Commissioner Trainor's trip to Romania at the following links:
The reports are published on OGE's website here: USOGE | News<https://www.oge.gov/web/OGE.nsf/All%20travel%20reports>
Expenses for Mr. Trainor's trip to Romania are in this report: This travel report covers the period between April 1, 2025 - September 30, 2025.<https://www.oge.gov/Web/OGE.nsf/All%20Travel%20Reports/9E6618CC22DB466385258D55006756B8/$FILE/FEC%201353%20Travel%20Report%20-%20Apr.%201%20-%20Sep.%2030%20(2025).pdf?open>
              We are withholding approximately 217 documents under Exemptions 5 and 6.
Exemption 5 protects from disclosure inter- or intra-agency memoranda or letters that would not be available by law to a party other than an agency in litigation with the agency, including documents covered by the attorney work-product, deliberative process, and attorney-client privileges.  See 5 U.S.C. § 552(b)(5).
Exemption 6 protects personal information, the disclosure of which would constitute a clearly unwarranted invasion of personal privacy.  See 5 U.S.C. § 552(b)(6).
Therefore, your FOIA request has been granted in part and denied in part. Please note that the Agency considers the foreseeable harm standard when reviewing records and applying FOIA exemptions to maximize releases to requesters. Additionally, the Agency's response to your request does not include documents or publications publicly available on our website or compilations of publicly available news articles.
You may contact our FOIA Public Liaison, Amber Smith at foia@fec.gov for any further assistance and to discuss any aspect of your request.  Additionally, you may contact the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) at the National Archives and Records Administration to inquire about the FOIA mediation services they offer.  The contact information for OGIS is as follows: Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road-OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001, e-mail at ogis@nara.gov<mailto:ogis@nara.gov>; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448; or facsimile at 202-741-5769.

You may appeal any adverse FOIA determination.  Any such appeal must be filed in writing and should follow the guidelines set forth in 11 C.F.R. § 4.8.  If you have any questions, please contact the FOIA Requester Service Center at FOIA@fec.gov<mailto:FOIA@fec.gov>, or (202) 694-1650.

Sincerely,

FOIA Requester Service Center

title: CPRA Request: Flock Safety ALPR Records

communications.subject: California Public Records Act Request: CPRA Request: Flock Safety ALPR Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Please provide copies of the following records from January 1, 2024, to the date of this request, unless a different timeframe is specified below:

I. Contracts, Funding, and Acquisition
Please provide documents for Items 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5 from the period between the inception of the agency's relationship with Flock Group, Inc. (Flock Safety) and the present.
1. All contracts, licensing agreements, software-as-a-service (SaaS) subscriptions, Master Services Agreements (MSA), and Memoranda of Understanding (MOU) between the Belmont Police Department (or the City of Belmont acting on its behalf) and Flock Group, Inc. (Flock Safety).
2. Any amendments, renewals, extensions, or change orders to the agreements described in Item 1.
3. All purchase orders, invoices, and records of payments made to Flock Safety.
4. Records identifying the specific funding sources used for these systems (e.g., General Fund, asset forfeiture funds, state/federal grants, or private donations).
5. All internal memos, proposals, or staff reports discussing the acquisition, approval, placement, or pilot programs for Flock ALPR cameras.

II. Policy, Privacy, and Governance
6. The current ALPR Usage and Privacy Policy, as required by California Civil Code § 1798.90.51, including data retention schedules and access protocols.
7. Annual ALPR audit reports required by Civil Code § 1798.90.53.
8. Training materials, policies, or directives provided to sworn and civilian personnel regarding authorized use of the Flock system.
9. Any Surveillance Use Policies, Anticipated Surveillance Impact Reports, or Privacy Impact Assessments related to ALPR technology generally or Flock Safety specifically.
10. The Belmont Police Department's records about agendas, minutes, or logs of meetings regarding the contracting, implementation, or security of Flock Safety cameras.
11. Documentation regarding public notification of surveillance, including signage standards, maps provided to the public, or press releases.
12. Any complaints or grievances received from the public regarding ALPR camera placement or data use.

III. Operational and Technical Data
13. A list of all current and planned locations of Flock Safety ALPR cameras deployed by the Belmont Police Department (including those in unincorporated areas and contract cities), including: coordinates or intersection descriptions, installation dates, the specific purpose for each location (e.g., traffic monitoring, crime prevention), camera model numbers/hardware specifications.
14. The names and titles of the specific System Administrators or managers responsible for the oversight of the Flock Safety system (e.g., Professional Services Division Commander).

IV. Usage Data, Sharing, and Audits
15. The total number of license plates scanned (read) by the Belmont Police Department's Flock cameras, aggregated by month.
16. All existing audit or access logs generated by the Flock Safety system documenting queries/searches conducted by agency personnel. I request that personally identifiable information of non-employee subjects be redacted rather than the records withheld. Please include: date and time of search, reason for search (as entered into the system audit trail), and the agency or unit of the officer conducting the search.
17. Records regarding the sharing of ALPR data with the Northern California Regional Intelligence Center (NCRIC), including but not limited to: 
- Any MOUs, data-sharing agreements, or network-participation terms governing the transmission of the Belmont Police Department's ALPR data to NCRIC; 
- Records describing the technical mechanism by which data is shared (e.g., automatic real-time feed, batch upload, or query-based access);
- Any policies, directives, or written guidance governing what restrictions, if any, the Belmont Police Department places on NCRIC's downstream use or further dissemination of Belmont Police Department-origin ALPR data;
- Records of any requests received from NCRIC (or through NCRIC from other agencies) to query or obtain Belmont Police Department ALPR data, and the Belmont Police Department's responses to such requests.
18. A list of all "hot lists" (e.g., stolen vehicles, Amber alerts) or custom "hot lists" currently integrated into the Belmont Police Department Flock system.
19. A list of all outside agencies (local, state, and federal) that the Belmont Police Department shares Flock data with, and a list of all outside agencies from whom the Belmont Police Department receives Flock data, including but not limited to access provided through Flock Safety's real-time network sharing features (e.g., the platform previously known as TALON), and any data-sharing agreements, MOUs, or network participation terms governing such access.
20. Any statistical reports or evaluations generated by the department or Flock Safety regarding the efficacy of the system (e.g., number of vehicles recovered, arrests made attributable to ALPR hits).
21. Records of any unauthorized access, data breaches, or security incidents involving ALPR data.

V. Communication
22. All written communications (emails and attachments) between Belmont Police Department employees and representatives of Flock Safety (email domain @flocksafety.com), excluding routine IT troubleshooting tickets.
23. All written communications with other law enforcement agencies or federal partners regarding requests to access or share Belmont Police Department Flock data.

If you determine that any portion of the requested records is exempt from disclosure, please redact only those portions and provide the remainder of the record. For any redaction or denial, please cite the specific legal exemption justifying the withholding, as required by Government Code § 7922.540.

Given that this request is made in the public interest by a student journalist writing for Scot Scoop, I respectfully request a waiver or reduction of any fees associated with this request.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Maximus Kwan

title: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (State of Texas Secretary of State, Elections Division)

communications.subject: Texas Public Information Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (State of Texas Secretary of State, Elections Division)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Texas Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Any and all memoranda of understanding (MOUs), agreements, contracts, or similar instruments between the state and the U.S. Department of Justice relating to the provision of voter registration data, voter files, or voter roll information, from January 20, 2025 to the present.

2. Any draft, unsigned, proposed, or rejected versions of such agreements.

3. Any amendments, addenda, or modifications to such agreements.

This request includes but is not limited to any document described or labeled as a "memorandum of understanding" or contract relating to voter data, voter list maintenance, or voter roll verification.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

communications.subject: RE: Texas Public Information Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Agreements (State of Texas Secretary of State, Elections Division)

To Whom It May Concern:

communications.subject: Acknowledgment Email - 2026-FOIA-00125
communications.communication:
Case No: 2026-FOIA-00125 
 
December 2, 2025 
Dear MuckRock News DEPT MR 197571: This email acknowledges receipt of your request, case tracking number 2026-FOIA-00125.  Your request is assigned a case tracking number based on the time it was received by FOIAXpress.  Please know that due to the government shutdown,FOIA requests were not processed from October 1, 2025 through November 12, 2025.  The government reopened on November 13,2025.  We appreciate your patience as weresume FOIA case processing. 
The FOIA is centrally processed at the Social Security Administration (SSA) within the Office of Privacy and Disclosure with a subset of requests – requests for copies of decedents’ original SS-5 and Numident records – processed by the Office of Central Operations, Division of Earnings and Business Services.  Please know that FOIA requests are categorized as either simple or complex, depending on the nature of the request and the estimated processing time.  For most non-expedited simple requests, we make a determination about release of the record(s) requested within 20 business days.  We will place into a complex processing queue any request that cannot be completed within 20 business days due to unusual circumstances. 

If you need to correspond with us regarding your request, you may leave a voicemail by calling 410-965-1727 or you may send email correspondence to our FOIA Public Lia ison at FOIA.Public.Liaison@ssa.gov .  When you email this mailbox, we encourage you to limit the amount of personally identifiable information you provide in your email correspondence.  While SSA operates within a secure network, we have no control of the data we receive while it is in transit to or from our FOIA Public Liaison mailbox.  Please include your case tracking number in your email. Sincerely, 
The FOIA and Privacy Act Team 
SSA

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request #2026-FOIA-00125 - Status
communications.communication:
Dear Sir or Madam, 

The records you seekrequire the need for consultation with multiple components of the agency.  Because of these unusual circumstances, weneed to extend the time limit to respond to your request. See 5U.S.C. § 552(a)(6)(B)(iii). 
No later than January 26, 2026, you will receive further correspondence from us. In accordance with 5 U.S.C. §552(a)(6)(B)(ii), because we require more than ten additional working days toprocess your request, you have the opportunity to:  (1) modify or narrowthe scope of your request so that it can be processed within the required timeperiod, and/or (2) arrange an alternate time frame for processing your originalrequest or modified request.  If you wish to pursue either of theseoptions, you must notify us. 

Sincerely, 
The FOIA Team, SSA

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request #2026-FOIA-00125 - Status 01-22-2026

Dear Requester, 

We are continuing to process your request but require additional time.  No later than February 23, 2026 you will receive further correspondence from us. 

Please do not reply to this email correspondence, i.e., to this email address.  If you have an account withFOIAonline, you can correspond directly with us by logging into youraccount.  If you need to correspond with us regarding your FOIA requestbut do not have an account with FOIAonline, please send your correspondence to FOIA.Public.Liaison@ssa.gov.  The FOIA Public Liaison mailbox wascreated to assist the public with their FOIA requests, as well as to provideassistance to individuals requesting access to their own records under thePrivacy Act.  When you email thismailbox, we encourage you to limit the amount of personally identifiableinformation you provide in your email correspondence.  While the Social Security Administration (SSA)operates within a secure network, we have no control of the data we receivewhile it is in transit to or from our FOIA Public Liaison mailbox.  Please include your FOIA tracking number inyour email. (mailto:FOIA.Public.Liaison@ssa.gov) 
Sincerely, 
The FOIA Team, SSA

title: Georgia Records Request

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Georgia Records Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all documents that mention Cordavis possessed by staffers working for Department of Community Health's State Health Benefit Plan dated between August 2023 and the date of fulfillment of this request. The documents sought should include but not be limited to (introduced here only as illustrative examples) email communications, memoranda, meeting notes, letters, text messages, social media communications, video meeting recordings.

Definitions
 
“Any” and “all” mean all, each, any, and every.
 
“Document(s)” means any and all written, typed, printed, recorded or graphic matter, however produced, reproduced or stored, whether an original or a copy, and whether prepared, published or released by any person or entity, including but not limited to letters, reports, agreements, correspondence, intra-office or inter-office correspondence, telegrams, minutes or records of meetings, reports or summaries, expressions or statements, lists, drafts and revisions, invoices, receipts, original and preliminary notes, sketches, records, ledgers, contracts, bills of lading, bills, inventories, financial data, maps, memoranda, accounting and financial records, diaries, journals, calendars, statements, work papers, videotapes, photographs, pamphlets, brochures, advertisements, trade letters, press releases, drawings, recaps, tables, articles, summaries of conversations, computer cards, tapes, diskettes, or other means of electronically or magnetically maintaining information, and printouts. 
 
The term “document(s)” also includes electronically stored data from which information can be obtained either directly or by translation through detection devices or readers; any such document is to be produced in a reasonably legible and usable form. The term “document(s)” includes all drafts of a document and all copies that differ in any respect from the original, including any notation, underlining, marking, or information not on the original. The term also includes information stored in, or accessible through, computer or other information retrieval systems (including any computer archives or back-up systems), together with instructions and all other materials necessary to use or interpret such data compilations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Steve Horn

communications.subject: Acknowledgement of Open Records Request # 64421
communications.communication:
The Georgia Department of Community Health (DCH) acknowledges receipt of your Open Records Act Request #64421 which was received by the Office of General Counsel on 11/10/2025. You have requested the following for date ranges 1/8/2023 to 11/10/2025 :

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any and all documents that mention Cordavis possessed by staffers working for Department of Community Health's State Health Benefit Plan dated between August 2023 and the date of fulfillment of this request. The documents sought should include but not be limited to (introduced here only as illustrative examples) email communications, memoranda, meeting notes, letters, text messages, social media communications, video meeting recordings.

Definitions

“Any” and “all” mean all, each, any, and every.

“Document(s)” means any and all written, typed, printed, recorded or graphic matter, however produced, reproduced or stored, whether an original or a copy, and whether prepared, published or released by any person or entity, including but not limited to letters, reports, agreements, correspondence, intra-office or inter-office correspondence, telegrams, minutes or records of meetings, reports or summaries, expressions or statements, lists, drafts and revisions, invoices, receipts, original and preliminary notes, sketches, records, ledgers, contracts, bills of lading, bills, inventories, financial data, maps, memoranda, accounting and financial records, diaries, journals, calendars, statements, work papers, videotapes, photographs, pamphlets, brochures, advertisements, trade letters, press releases, drawings, recaps, tables, articles, summaries of conversations, computer cards, tapes, diskettes, or other means of electronically or magnetically maintaining information, and printouts.

The term “document(s)” also includes electronically stored data from which information can be obtained either directly or by translation through detection devices or readers; any such document is to be produced in a reasonably legible and usable form. The term “document(s)” includes all drafts of a document and all copies that differ in any respect from the original, including any notation, underlining, marking, or information not on the original. The term also includes information stored in, or accessible through, computer or other information retrieval systems (including any computer archives or back-up systems), together with instructions and all other materials necessary to use or interpret such data compilations.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Steve Horn

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2127607/



Open Records has routed your request to the Division(s) who may have responsive records. Your request is being reviewed to preliminarily determine whether any records may exist that would be responsive. Under the Georgia Open Records Act (GORA) at O.C.G.A. §50-18-71(b)(1)(A), the statute provides that if responsive records exist but are unavailable within three business days, the agency is to provide the requester with a description of such records and a timeline for when the records will be available. At this time, we are determining whether responsive records exist. In the event such records do exist, we anticipate providing you with a timeline to respond to your request by 11/17/2025, and each week thereafter until such request is fulfilled. Should these records become available sooner they will be reviewed and supplied to you shortly there-after.

Please be advised that the Open Records Act has a provision that allows an agency to charge for the production of records. Pursuant to O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(d), DCH will provide a cost estimate and obtain your confirmation to pay the charges before finalizing the request if the cost to produce the records is in excess of $25.00.

Please continue to correspond with us via the Open Records Mailbox (openrecordsrequest@dch.ga.gov) so that we may better assist you by keeping all interested parties up to date on this matter.

Thank you for your patience. Please advise if I can be of further assistance.

title: Dorian Johnson records (Federal Bureau of Investigation)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Dorian Johnson records (Federal Bureau of Investigation)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Any investigatory files related to Dorian Johnson, the Ferguson, Mo. man who was witness to the 2014 fatal police shooting of Michael Brown, who died in Sept. 2025. His death was widely reported, here's a reference link: https://www.nytimes.com/2025/09/08/us/dorian-johnson-ferguson-michael-brown.html

I also request a search of the Electronic Surveillance (ELSUR) Index and the COINTELPRO Index, or any similar technique for locating records of electronic surveillance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Wesley Lowery

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Sincerely,

Wesley Lowery

title: IL SoS communications (Forest Park)

communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: IL SoS communications (Forest Park)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

On September 5, 2025, Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias issued a press release announcing that their audit uncovered that the Forest Park Police Department was sharing its license plate reader (LPR) data with the department of Customs and Border Protection. I am seeking email communications related to this issue. Specifically, I am seeking the following:

1. Emails with the Illinois Secretary of State's office
- Copies of all emails (including any attachments),
- Sent or received (including CC & BCC fields) by the Forest Park Mayor and Police Chief, 
- To or from any sender email address containing the domain @ilsos.gov,
- between 08/1/2025 and the 10/31/2025  (inclusive of those dates).

2. Emails with Motorola
- Copies of all emails (including any attachments),
- Sent or received (including CC & BCC fields) by the Forest Park Mayor and Police Chief, 
- To or from any sender email address containing the domain @motorolasolutions.com,  
- Between 08/1/2025 and the 10/31/2025  (inclusive of those dates).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

RTL

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: IL SoS communications (Forest Park)
communications.communication:
Hello – I will look into this FOIA.
Can you please confirm where the February 28 request was sent as it was not received to this email and it is not visible on the email below.
I will need an additional 5 days to gather relevant information. I will respond no later than March 18, 2026.
Please confirm receipt. The February 28 FOIA request was not received at our office. Only the FOIA today, March 4 was received.
Thank you.
Megan Roach
FOIA Officer

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: IL SoS communications (Forest Park)
communications.communication:
I have a clarification for this request. 
I am seeking the described emails sent or received (including CC & BCC fields) by the Forest Park Mayor *OR* the Police Chief. I am seeking the emails for one or the other people, not combined. 

I apologize if my initial request was unclear. Thank you for your assistance with this request.
RTL

communications.subject: RE: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: IL SoS communications (Forest Park)
communications.communication:
Megan Roach, 
I apologize, I don't know where Muckrock routed the initial request. Regardless, I confirm that the receive date for your department was today, March 4 and I will patiently await a response.
For clarity, I will restate the request, inclusive of the adjustment I previously communicated. 
Thank you for your assistance with this request.

==== FOIA REQUEST ====
Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:
On September 5, 2025, Secretary of State Alexi Giannoulias issued a press release announcing that their audit uncovered that the Forest Park Police Department was sharing its license plate reader (LPR) data with the department of Customs and Border Protection. I am seeking email communications related to this issue. Specifically, I am seeking the following:

1. Emails with the Illinois Secretary of State's office
- Copies of all emails (including any attachments),
- Sent or received (including CC & BCC fields) by the Forest Park Mayor or Police Chief,
- To or from any sender email address containing the domain @ilsos.gov,
- between 08/1/2025 and the 10/31/2025 (inclusive of those dates).

2. Emails with Motorola
- Copies of all emails (including any attachments),
- Sent or received (including CC & BCC fields) by the Forest Park Mayor or Police Chief,
- To or from any sender email address containing the domain @motorolasolutions.com,
- Between 08/1/2025 and the 10/31/2025 (inclusive of those dates).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All current "Brady lists" and "Giglio lists" (per Brady v. Maryland/Giglio v. United States) maintained by your office, along with accompanying potential impeachment material.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

If you believe that there are other records in existence that should have been released to you in response to this request, please notify the Public Records Office as soon as possible. Pursuant Clallam County Policy 321.17, fees for records requests shall be waived when the total fee for the request is under ten dollars. Since the total fee for this request is under ten dollars, no payment is due at this time. title: CM Hollingsworth - AI Usage

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: CM Hollingsworth - AI Usage
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

All text, images, and videos generated by CM Hollingsworth for City business using City-owned or personal generative AI software, as well as corresponding prompts used to generate them.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Rose Terse

Dear Rose Terse, 
On December 16, 2025, the Legislative Department received your public records request C249856-121625. 
Your request: To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records: 

All text, images, and videos generated by CM Hollingsworth for City business using City-owned or personal generative AI software, as well as corresponding prompts used to generate them. 

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes. 

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2156983/ 
A reasonable search of my department, including having custodians search non-City emails and texts, personal One Drive and hard drive, did not locate responsive records. The Legislative Department considers your request closed and plans to take no further action to fulfill this request. 
If you have any follow-up questions or would like to initiate an administrative appeal under MDAR 17-0002 Section 6, please contact us within 10 business days, after which this request will no longer be monitored for communications. You may also seek judicial review of our response to your request for a period of one year, beginning as of the date of this message, consistent with RCW 42.56.550. 
Sincerely, 
Ann Vuong 
(206) 233-3896 
Legislative Department

communications.communication:
Hi Ann,

Thank you for the quick response. I was previously provided records including this clearly AI-generated image by CM Hollingsworth's office. Would you please double-check that you have conducted a thorough search?

Happy holidays and have a wonderful new year!

Thank you,
Rose

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not. 

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires. 

Sincerely, 

Rose Terse 

Upload documents directly: https://www.muckrock.com/respond/2156983/ 
The records being made available to you at this time include records that have been confirmed to be responsive to your request. The installment fee has again been waived. I worked with the Council office to obtain these responsive records. They did, however, use a free account so the prompts were not saved. 
To download your records, please: 
 
  * Navigate to the Public Records Request Center. 
  * Select 'My Records Request Center'. 
  * Select 'View My Requests'. You may be asked to login. 
  * Select this request: C249856-121625. 
  * Select the 'View Files' button. 
  * You can download records individually or all at the same time by clicking 'Download All'. 
  *  Please save your records as you may only download them from the system three times. If you do not download your records within 30 days of request closure, the link to your records will expire. 
If you believe you have received privileged or confidential records in error, please notify the City of Seattle immediately, through the Public Records Request Center or by email. Privileged or confidential records provided or received in error should not be reviewed, used, disseminated, distributed, or copied, and should be destroyed immediately. Thank you in advance for your cooperation. 
The Legislative Department has now provided all responsive records. We consider your request closed and plan to take no further action to fulfill this request. If you have any follow-up questions or would like to initiate an administrative appeal under MDAR 17-0002 Section 6, please contact us within 10 business days after downloading your records, after which this request will no longer be monitored for communications. You may also seek judicial review of our response to your request for a period of one year, beginning as of the date of this message, consistent with RCW 42.56.550. 
Sincerely, 
Ann Vuong 
(206) 233-3896 
Legislative Department

title: Request to Navy for all 2025 film and TV collaborations

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to Navy for all 2025 film and TV collaborations
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

A list of all the films and television productions that the U.S. Navy has supported, in any capacity whatsoever, during the year 2025. Such a list, full or partial, maybe include, but should not limited to, entertainment liaison (ELO) reports or similar reports. I request that you search for these documents in any location where they might be located, including but not limited to the entertainment liaison offices and communications HQ.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

communications.communication:
This will acknowledge receipt of your letter received *January 13, 2026*,
wherein you requested information pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of
Information Act.


Please be advised that the City of Danbury is firmly committed to providing
prompt access to all records subject to disclosure under Connecticut
General Statutes §1-210, et. seq.  Accordingly, all documents will be
assembled and reviewed as quickly as reasonably possible in light of other
responsibilities and immediate commitments.  Although the City of Danbury
takes your request seriously and will respond as promptly as possible, we
reserve the right to petition the Freedom of Information Commission for a
declaratory ruling regarding requests that may be exempt from disclosure.



Please note, that there will be a charge of $.50 per page, and any amount
of over $10.00 must be paid in advance.



 If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact our office.

title: Cost to Acquire SS Simmons Victory and to Convert it to the USS Liberty (AGTR-5)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Cost to Acquire SS Simmons Victory and to Convert it to the USS Liberty (AGTR-5)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

In the 1960’s the US government acquired the SS Simmons Victory and converted it to the USS Liberty (AGTR-5).

We are requesting the documents related to the price the US government paid for the SS Simmons Victory and the cost the US government paid to convert the ship into the USS Liberty (AGTR-5).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Joe Meadors

request states:

*In the 1960’s the US government acquired the SS Simmons Victory and
converted it to the USS Liberty (AGTR-5).  We are requesting the documents
related to the price the US government paid for the SS Simmons Victory and
the cost the US government paid to convert the ship into the USS Liberty
(AGTR-5).*

Our office handles FOIA and Privacy Act requests for NARA’s operational records
(records created by this agency).  Because your FOIA request is for records

different agency but that may have been transferred to the legal custody of
NARA), we are forwarding it by this email to our Office of Research

communications.subject: Re: [26-27749] Freedom of Information Act Request - Joe Meadors
communications.communication:
Dear Joe Meadors,


This is in response to your Freedom of Information Act request dated
February 13, 2026 for records relating to the purchase and overhaul of the
SS Simmons Victory which occurred on the approximate dates of early 1960s.
Your request was received in this office, A2 Textual Reference, on the date
of February 19, 2026 and assigned FOIA case number 26-27749.


We have conducted a records review within Record Group 344: Records of
Naval Sea Systems Command and we have located records that may be
potentially relevant to your research. During our review we searched for
terms such as USS Liberty, AGTR-5, SS Simmons Victory, and variations
thereof. During our review we located Ship Departure Reports within Record
Group 344, Entry UD-04W 2, Series: Office of Management Services, Ship
Departure Reports, 1963-1968, File; AGTR 5. Additionally, we conducted a
review of Record Group 19: Records of the Navy's Bureau of Ships for
correspondence records, during our review we noted that correspondence
records for the Bureau of Ships dated 1962-1964 were not provided by the
Department of the Navy. We did not locate references to the SS Simmons
Victory or USS Liberty (AGTR-5) in the correspondence records dated 1961. The
materials you are requesting are already open for public viewing in our
research room at the National Archives in College Park, MD, located at 8601
Adelphi Road, College Park, MD. We are staffed to assist researchers with
their work by providing onsite reference services. Since there are no
access restrictions on these documents, there is no need to file a Freedom
of Information Act request with the National Archives.


We will be happy to make the records and their finding aids available to
you or your representative in the Textual Research Room (Room 2000) here at
the National Archives at College Park, Maryland. Please visit our website
for information about visiting the National Archives in College Park, MD
<https://www.archives.gov/college-park>, including how to schedule a
research visit. If you are unable to visit our research reading rooms you
may consider hiring a private researcher to conduct research on your
behalf. The scope of your research, combined with our staffing limitations
may make this the most efficient way to provide you with professional
research services. A list of private researchers familiar with National
Archives facilities is available on our Independent Researchers Available
for Hire <https://www.archives.gov/research/hire-help> webpage.


This concludes the processing of your request. We do not consider this a
denial of your FOIA request. If you are not satisfied with our action on
this request, your options for appeal and dispute resolution are attached.


We hope you find this information useful in conducting your research.

Sincerely,


JACOB A. HAYWOOD

Textual Reference Archives II Branch (RRAR)

National Archives at College Park, MD
