title: Flock Safety Records

communications.subject: The Missouri Sunshine Law Request: Flock Safety Records
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the The Missouri Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

1) I request internal training materials for the use of "Flock Safety" tools.

2) I request policies and procedures for the use of Flock tools

3) I request any sharing agreements signed with other agencies as well as department's sharing agreement it requires other agencies to conform to.

4) I request the web address of the department's "Flock Safety" transparency portal if activated. This is a free feature of Flock. This is not an add-on or analytics subscription.

5) Lastly, I request all available audit data from the "Flock" audit tools. This should include the organization audit, network audit, and sharing report.
    A. Organization Audit in Flock.
    The report should include data logged from the period of installation to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done by the City.

    B. Network Audit in Flock.
    The report should include data logged from the period of installation, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the City’s Flock network by any agency the City has shared with in the Flock System.

    C. Sharing report in Flock.
    The report should include flock networks shared with The City and networks The City is sharing its network with.

Flock maintains documentation on these reports. Flock documentation says the reports can be pulled in 31 day increments.  Flock also says that customers may want to contact support for requests longer than six months or for large accounts. If production of audit logs requires support from Flock, please declare such needs and have the City's point person on the Flock support ticket provide updates on their progress.  Please note that license plate numbers are public information or it would be unsafe to display them. Drivers would also be able to hide their plates in their trunk if they were meant to be private.  Officer usernames are similarly publicly available by many resources, such as news reports, court cases, case notes, and reports.

I request that files be supplied in their original electronic, machine readable format rather than being converted to any alternate formats. Where multiple formats are available for export, I request csv, xls, or other spreadsheet compatible formats.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael

communications.communication:
Good Day,
I am following up on this request originally submitted on 1/23/2026.  To date, I have received no communication in response to this request.  This seems completely improper as the sunshine law statute allows three business days for a response to a request.  Under the law, production of records may take longer, but a detailed explanation for the delay and the earliest date and time records will be available must be shared.

With this being the case, I am requesting an immediate explanation for the delay in responding to this request and the earliest date and time the records I requested will be available.

Thank you,
Michael

communications.subject: Public Records Request :: R023265-022426

Crystal Higdon has been contacted numerous times as seen on this request.  This request was submitted on 1/23/26. Crystal Higdon has so far not responded.  Section 610.023.3, RSMo states that requests are to be responded to within three business days.  The law does not allow for a government agency to dictate the manner in which the request arrives.

title: Request to Navy for 2019 budget report of Public Affairs Office

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Request to Navy for 2019 budget report of Public Affairs Office
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

The annual budget report for the Navy's Public Affairs Office for the year 2019.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: Open Records Act Request for Records on Bar Eligibility, Immigration Status Requirements, and Investigations Involving Emory University law students (JD and LLM), including American domestic, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmigrant students (SEVIS-tracked) Student

communications.subject: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Act Request for Records on Bar Eligibility, Immigration Status Requirements, and Investigations Involving Emory University law students (JD and LLM), including American domestic, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmigran
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Subject: Open Records Act Request for Records on Bar Eligibility, Immigration Status Requirements, and Investigations Involving Emory University law students (JD and LLM), including American domestic, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmigrant students (SEVIS-tracked) Students (2015–Present)
Dear OBA Custodian of Records:
Pursuant to the Georgia Open Records Act, O.C.G.A. § 50-18-70 et seq., MuckRock requests this inspection and copies of the following public records:
1.	All applications, determinations, or denials of bar eligibility under Ga. Bar R. 4(c) for Emory University law students (JD and LLM), including American domestic, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmigrant students (SEVIS-tracked) students, 2015–present, including those impacted by SEVIS terminations or immigration status.
2.	Correspondence, memos, or data between OBA and Emory University (Law School/ISSS), DHS/SEVP, or AG on bar access barriers for international students, including 2025 SEVIS effects.
3.	Aggregated, anonymized statistics on bar applicants from Emory/Georgia law schools denied due to status (undocumented, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmigrant students (SEVIS-tracked)), 2015–present; outcomes/trends; including disparities data vs. ABA policies (e.g., Res. 108, 2017, on undocumented eligibility), empirical evidence of denials/waivers, and communications with Emory on transcript/funding issues.
4.	All OBA/State Bar/Supreme Court reports, statistics, or empirical evidence (e.g., case summaries, waiver denials) on undocumented/visa law student bar eligibility/fitness 2015–2025, including disparities vs. ABA policies (e.g., Res. 108, 2017; 2022 reforms), COVID waivers (Special Rule 2020-1), and communications with Emory on transcript/funding issues.
This disclosure is in the public interest because abrupt loss of lawful status without due process constitutes a severe liberty deprivation under Zadvydas v. Davis, 533 U.S. 678 (2001), governed by the Mathews v. Eldridge, 424 U.S. 319 (1976) balancing test, and recently recognized in Liu v. Noem (D.N.H. 2025) and Kuck Baxter v. DHS (N.D. Ga. 2025).
A complete fee waiver or reduction under O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(g) is requested because this request involves a matter of substantial public concern — the deprivation of due process and effective banishment of nonimmigrant students through no-notice SEVIS terminations (see Zadvydas v. Davis, 533 U.S. 678 (2001); Mathews v. Eldridge, 424 U.S. 319 (1976); Liu v. Noem, D.N.H. 2025; Kuck Baxter v. DHS, N.D. Ga. 2025).
Expedited processing is requested under O.C.G.A. § 50-18-71(b)(2) due to imminent irreparable harm from loss of lawful status and ongoing litigation (Kuck Baxter v. DHS, N.D. Ga. 2025). 
Provide within 3 business days; electronic PDF format preferred. No search fees (Trammell v. Martin, 261 Ga. 1 (1991)).
If any fees are assessed despite the waiver request, please provide an itemized quote; payment will be made via prepaid gift card or U.S. Postal money order to preserve anonymity.
Thank you.
Sincerely,
MuckRock News on behalf of Anonymous Requester

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

neutralrequest47

communications.subject: Re: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Act Request for Records on Bar Eligibility, Immigration Status Requirements, and Investigations Involving Emory University law students (JD and LLM), including American domestic, F-1, J-1, and M-1 noni...
communications.communication:
Good Morning!
The Office of Bar Admissions is an arm of the Supreme Court of Georgia which is part of the judicial branch of government and therefore are not included within the scope of the Georgia Open Records Act.  O.C.G.A. §§ 50-18-70(a), 50-14-1(a)(1); Fathers are Parents Too, Inc. v. Hunstein, 202 Ga. App. 716 (1992); 1979 Op. Att’y Gen 79-25.
Have a great week!


Rebecca S. Mick

Assistant Director

Office of Bar Admissions

Nathan Deal Judicial Center
330 Capitol Avenue, SE
Suite L200
Atlanta, GA  30334

404-656-2003

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Act Request for Records on Bar Eligibility, Immigration Status Requirements, and Investigations Involving Emory University law students (JD and LLM), including American domestic, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmi
communications.communication:
Dear Rebecca S. Mick,
Thank you for your response dated December 9, 2025.
The position that the Office of Bar Admissions falls outside the general scope of the Georgia Open Records Act (“GORA”), O.C.G.A. §§ 50-18-70(a), 50-14-1(a)(1), as interpreted in Fathers Are Parents Too, Inc. v. Hunstein, 202 Ga. App. 716 (1992), and 1979 Op. Att’y Gen. 79-25 because Office of Bar Admissions operates as an arm of the Supreme Court of Georgia is, respectfully, irrelevant for the purposes of this request.
Respectfully, this request seeks administrative and statistical records, not adjudicative case files, and concerns matters of public administration rather than judicial decision-making.
Specifically, the request seeks:
1.	 Aggregated, anonymized statistical data
2.	Administrative correspondence regarding eligibility standards
3.	Policy guidance concerning immigration-status considerations
4.	Reports or empirical summaries concerning bar eligibility determinations
These categories involve the administrative operation of a professional licensing authority rather than confidential adjudicative deliberations in individual fitness cases.
Even where judicial entities are exempt from GORA as courts, Georgia law recognizes a distinction between adjudicative functions and administrative functions. Records reflecting administrative policy development, statistical reporting, or inter-agency communications are not judicial opinions or case files and therefore do not implicate judicial decisional independence.
Importantly, this is not a request for:
1.	Confidential character-and-fitness investigative files of specific applicants
2.	Deliberative materials of pending matters
3.	Personally identifying information of third parties
Rather, the request is limited to:
1.	Aggregate data
2.	Policy-level communications
3.	Administrative reports
4.	Non-case-specific determinations affecting categories of applicants
If the Office maintains that GORA does not apply in any respect, I respectfully request clarification as to whether:
1.	The Supreme Court of Georgia maintains any public-access mechanism for administrative records of the Office of Bar Admissions;
2.	Aggregated statistical data concerning eligibility determinations exists;
3.	Any portion of the request can be processed voluntarily in the interest of transparency.
If the denial rests on confidentiality under the Rules Governing Admission to the Practice of Law (Part F, Section 4), I respectfully note that confidentiality provisions protect individual applicant records and reputational interests; they do not preclude disclosure of anonymized statistical data or general policy communications.
I appreciate your time and look forward to your clarification regarding the proper procedural mechanism for obtaining administrative records, if not under GORA.
Sincerely,
neutralrequest47

communications.subject: Re: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Act Request for Records on Bar Eligibility, Immigration Status Requirements, and Investigations Involving Emory University law students (JD and LLM), including American domestic, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmi
communications.communication:
Thank you for the clarification.  All of the information relating to your requests can be found on our website which you can access here<https://www.gabaradmissions.org/home>. Use the yellow ribbon at the top to explore different subjects such as Rules & Policies and Admission Types.  You can also use the search bar to pull up pages specific to your interest.  We do not create reports or have the statistical data you are requesting other than what is on our website.  All other documentation relating to this office is considered confidential by our Rules.  I hope this helps!




Rebecca S. Mick

Assistant Director

Office of Bar Admissions

Nathan Deal Judicial Center
330 Capitol Avenue, SE
Suite L200
Atlanta, GA  30334

404-656-2003

communications.subject: RE: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Act Request for Records on Bar Eligibility, Immigration Status Requirements, and Investigations Involving Emory University law students (JD and LLM), including American domestic, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmi
communications.communication:
Dear Ms. Mick,
Subject: Clarification Regarding Administrative Records
Thank you for your prompt response.
I understand the Office’s position that it operates as an arm of the Supreme Court of Georgia and therefore may not be subject to the Georgia Open Records Act.
My request, however, concerns administrative and statistical records rather than individual applicant files or adjudicative deliberations.
To clarify, I respectfully ask:
1.	Whether aggregated statistical data concerning eligibility determinations exists in any format;
2.	Whether any annual administrative summaries or reports are maintained;
3.	Whether any public-facing policy memoranda address immigration-status considerations in bar eligibility.
If the Office maintains that no such aggregated or administrative records exist beyond the website materials referenced, I respectfully request written confirmation clarifying whether such records do not exist, are not maintained in the ordinary course of business, or are being withheld pursuant to a specific Rule or policy, with citation to the precise provision relied upon.
My intent is not to obtain confidential applicant information, but rather to understand the general administrative framework governing eligibility categories.
Thank you for your time and consideration.
Sincerely,
neutralrequest47

communications.subject: Re: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Act Request for Records on Bar Eligibility, Immigration Status Requirements, and Investigations Involving Emory University law students (JD and LLM), including American domestic, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmi

I wanted to follow up on the following Georgia Open Records Act request, copied below, and originally submitted on Dec. 8, 2025. Please let me know when I can expect to receive a response.

communications.subject: Re: Georgia Open Records Act Request: Open Records Act Request for Records on Bar Eligibility, Immigration Status Requirements, and Investigations Involving Emory University law students (JD and LLM), including American domestic, F-1, J-1, and M-1 nonimmi
communications.communication:
Hello,

Thank you for your follow-up inquiry. The Office of Bar Admissions operates as an arm of the Supreme Court of Georgia. Unfortunately, we are unable to fulfill your request for documents outside of those previously referenced by the former Assistant Director because the judicial branch falls outside the scope of Georgia's Open Records Act. See O.C.G.A. §§ 50-18-70(a); 50-14-1; Fathers are Parents Too v. Hunstein, 202 Ga. App. 716 (1992); 1979 Op. Att'y Gen. 79-25. We do not maintain public documents outside of the scope of those made accessible to the public through our website. In this regard, you can find the policy of the Board to Determine Fitness of Bar Applicants as it relates to immigration status here<https://www.gabaradmissions.org/policy-statement> (under Part F).

I hope that information is helpful to you.

With Best Regards,

Kevin

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Santa Rosa County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Santa Rosa County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Utah HHS response

communications.subject: Government Records Access and Management Act Request: Utah HHS response
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Government Records Access and Management Act, I hereby request the following records:

Communications and correspondence with the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services regarding the Aug. 26, 2025, HHS letter requesting the Utah Department of Health and  Human Services remove content from the state's Personal Responsibility Education Program curricula and program materials. For reference, the letter can be found here: https://acf.gov/sites/default/files/documents/main/08.26.25-Combined-PREP-Letters.pdf.

I also request internal communications between any Utah Department of Health and  Human Services employees about the aforementioned letter. If the content changes were made, I request a copy of the material before it was changed as well as a copy of the material after it was changed.

I additionally request that, if possible, fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, processed by a representative of the news media/press and is made in the process of news gathering and not for commercial usage.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Ileana Garnand

title: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (New Jersey Secretary of State)

communications.subject: New Jersey Open Public Records Act Request: DOJ Voter Data Request Communications (New Jersey Secretary of State)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the New Jersey Open Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

From January 20, 2025 to the present:

1. All correspondence, emails, letters, and written communications between the agency and the U.S. Department of Justice, including but not limited to the Civil Rights Division, the Office of the Attorney General, and the Executive Office for Immigration Review, relating to voter registration data, voter files, voter rolls, or voter list maintenance.

2. All internal communications within the agency (including emails, memoranda, and briefing materials) discussing, referencing, or responsive to any DOJ request for voter registration data or any proposed or executed memorandum of understanding with the DOJ regarding voter data.

3. Any lists, spreadsheets, databases, or other records received from the DOJ identifying specific voters for removal, investigation, or further review.

4. All records relating to the engagement of any third-party contractor in connection with DOJ voter data requests or voter list maintenance activities conducted pursuant to any agreement with the DOJ.

If any records are withheld in whole or in part, please identify the specific statutory exemption relied upon for each withholding.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 7 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Audrey Nielsen

communications.subject: Government Records Request to New Jersey Department of State (DOJ Voter Data Agreements (New Jersey Secretary of State))
communications.communication:
Good afternoon,

The New Jersey Department of State is in receipt of your Government Records Request submitted to Feedback.SOS@sos.nj.gov.  You requested in relevant part:

1. Any and all memoranda of understanding (MOUs), agreements, contracts, or similar instruments between the state and the U.S. Department of Justice relating to the provision of voter registration data, voter files, or voter roll information, from January 20, 2025 to the present.
2. Any draft, unsigned, proposed, or rejected versions of such agreements.
3. Any amendments, addenda, or modifications to such agreements.
This request includes but is not limited to any document described or labeled as a "memorandum of understanding" or contract relating to voter data, voter list maintenance, or voter roll verification.

Please see the attached record which is responsive to your request for a draft Memorandum of Understanding regarding the State's voter registration data.  There are no other responsive records maintained, held, or otherwise received by the New Jersey Department of State to your request.

This will officially close out your Government Records Request.

Sincerely,

    New Jersey Department of State OPRA

On Jan. 27, 2026:
Subject: Pennsylvania Right to Know Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Luzerne County Sheriff)
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Pennsylvania Right to Know Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: University of Minnesota Police LRAD use against protestors

communications.subject: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: University of Minnesota Police LRAD use against protestors
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Minnesota Government Data Practices Act, I hereby request the following records:

In February 2026, multiple independent, local, and national news outlets published photographs and reporting regarding so-called “noise demonstrations” occurring on or near the University of Minnesota campus. Reporting and photographic evidence indicate that the University of Minnesota Police Department (UMPD) responded to several of these events and, in at least some instances, deployed a Long-Range Acoustical Device (LRAD). Multiple publicly available images show an LRAD device connected to a UMPD vehicle.

Accordingly, I request the following records:
1. LRAD Acquisition Records: All purchase orders, invoices, receipts, contracts, grant documentation, vendor correspondence, and related procurement records for any LRAD devices currently or previously in the possession of UMPD, including but not limited to devices manufactured by LRAD Corporation / Genasys, and including models such as the LRAD 500X.
--
2. LRAD Training and Operational Materials: All manuals, training documents, lesson plans, presentations (including PowerPoint or equivalent), standard operating procedures, quick-reference guides, safety warnings, or other instructional materials that describe, govern, or advise UMPD personnel on the operation, deployment, configuration, volume settings, warnings, or tactical use of LRAD devices.
--
3. Policies Governing Use of Force and Crowd Control: All current and historical UMPD policies, directives, or guidelines governing: Use of force, Crowd control or protest response, Deployment of less-lethal or acoustic devices, Escalation or authorization requirements for specialized equipment.
--
4. Deployment and After-Action Records (February 2026)
Any after-action reports, incident reports, operational summaries, or internal reviews referencing LRAD use during protests or demonstrations in February 2026 (Feb 1 - Feb 9), including records that identify: Date(s) and location(s) of deployment, Authorizing personnel, Purpose and duration of use.
--
If any portion of this request is unclear or requires clarification to facilitate a timely response, I am happy to confer.

Thank you for your time and assistance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Alan J. Segal

communications.subject: Thank you for contacting the Department of Public Safety Re: Minnesota Government Data Practices Act Request: University of Minnesota Police LRAD use against protestors
communications.communication:
If this is an emergency or an urgent concern, please call 9-1-1.  If you witness or have a situation that needs immediate attention, please call 9-1-1. 

This account is not actively monitored.  Emails to the University of Minnesota Department of Public Safety are normally responded to within 2-3 business days.  If you require assistance sooner, please contact us at 612 624-COPS (2677). 

If you are emailing to report a crime, please either call us at 612 624-2677 or visit our department at 505 Washington Ave SE to file a police report.  Police reports are not accepted at this email address. 

For media inquiries, please contact University Public Relations at unews@umn.edu. (mailto:unews@umn.edu) 

Thank you.

You can submit a data request at umn.nextrequest.com.

Sincerely,
Shannon

communications.subject: Your University of Minnesota public records request #26-268 has been submitted.
communications.communication:
University of Minnesota ************************************************************************ Your record request #26-268 has been submitted successfully. Dear Requester: Thank you for using our online records management portal to request public records from the University of Minnesota. You can monitor the progress of your request by clicking on the blue button within this notification. Adding this email address for the Data Request Center will help to ensure future request related emails do not end up in your spam folder. You will receive an email when your request has been completed.  Our goal is to respond to as many requests from as many requesters as quickly as possible. To achieve that goal while fielding a high volume of requests, we prioritize based on the complexity of the request and/or volume of data requested. Targeted requests are typically completed more quickly. If your request is large and/or complex, we will contact you to discuss opportunities to focus your request and allow us to respond to it more efficiently. Again, thank you for using the Data Request Center. Sincerely,  Laurie Beyer-Kropuenske MGDPA Responsible Authority Chief Data Practices Compliance Officer ************************************************************************ Reply to this email or sign in to contact University of Minnesota.

RE: Records Request - Southwood Apartments, Richmond, VA – Environmental Site Assessment

Good day,

In keeping with the statutory requirements for service, please provide your physical address when submitting future requests.

Pursuant to the matter listed above, and in accordance with Code of Virginia § 2.2-3704 (of the Virginia Freedom of Information Act), please find the following, as outlined in your request, as it exists in the Department of Planning and Development Review (PDR).
As of this writing, beyond the attached, there exists no violation of applicable building or zoning codes for the cited address(es).
The City of Richmond provides an online tool for (chronological) information related to permitting (proposed and issued).  It is conducted on its proprietary Online Permit Portal (OPP).  Using this tool, simply enter the address number, direction (if applicable) and name, EXCLUDING (address) suffixes – ST, RD, AV, WAY, etc.  Click, ‘SEARCH’ , and all available information will populate the bottom of your screen (to the extent it exists).  This is a ‘real-time’ tool, which extends from 1991, to the point of this writing.

https://energov.richmondgov.com/EnerGov_Prod/SelfService/richmondvaprod#/home<https://url.avanan.click/v2/r01/___https:/energov.richmondgov.com/EnerGov_Prod/SelfService/richmondvaprod%23/home___.YXAzOnBhcnRuZXJlc2k6YTpvOjBmMjQyYTcyODY5MDZhMzhkNWQxOGRhMTBlMTUxZTk5Ojc6MTYyNTpiM2U2NDgyNDc5MWExODU0ZGEzM2ExYmVjY2Q4ZTA2MWMwYjIyZDQ4NTdjZTQ2YjAwMmNhZjA2N2E3ZmJlNGVlOmg6VDpO>  --  COR – Online Permit Portal

Please note: Typically, Phase I Environmental Assessments, relative to storage tanks and related matters (possibly spills, cleanups, testing, etc. for any historic property uses) reside under the purview of the Department of Fire – Office of the Fire Marshal for the City Of Richmond.
Items requested related to water lines, and sewer lines or laterals (and other related matters) will reside with the Departments Utilities, and Public Works, respectively.
Date of initial construction is provided online by the official holder of that record, (from) the Office of the Assessor of Real Estate - https://www.rva.gov/assessor-real-estate<https://url.avanan.click/v2/r01/___https:/www.rva.gov/assessor-real-estate___.YXAzOnBhcnRuZXJlc2k6YTpvOjBmMjQyYTcyODY5MDZhMzhkNWQxOGRhMTBlMTUxZTk5Ojc6MTk2NzpmMzFkNDhlY2Q2MWQ5NmFhYjJjNjAyZWYxZGZiOTA5OGVkMmE4YzM1YjEzYWE1MjcwNWMzOTg5ZWUyODE4ODFlOmg6VDpO> .
Matters outlined in your request related to Zoning (Variances, and otherwise) research will reside outside of the FOIA tool and will take their form through a request of a Zoning Confirmation Letter (ZCL) from the Office of Zoning Administration.
The cost of this facilitation is $126.81 (= $ 28.18 p/h X ½ hr. X 9 sites).  Remittance is due, payable to the City of Richmond, within ten (10) business days ‘after’ receipt of these fulfillment records (@ 900 East Broad ST, Rm 110, Richmond, VA 23219).
As always, we welcome this opportunity to assist you.
Sincerely,

Isaac Marks
Freedom of Information Officer
Bureau of Permits and Inspections
[cid:image001.jpg@01DC3DEA.C23DE4B0]
900 E. Broad Street, Room 110
Richmond, Virginia 23219

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Bossier Parish Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Louisiana Public Records Law Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Bossier Parish Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Louisiana Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 3 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

title: Malden Public Schools Budgets and Amplify Contracts

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Malden Public Schools Budgets and Amplify Contracts
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Any contracts between Malden Public Schools and Amplify Education, as well as the approved Malden Public Schools budgets from FY 2020, FY 2021, FY 2022, FY 2023, FY 2024, and FY 2025.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Avieana Rivera

communications.subject: Request# 2026-0099 : Request Submission Acceptance Confirmation
communications.communication:
Please DO NOT reply to this email !! Please use the email address of the sender at bottom of this email, in order to communicate with the request management team. 
Malden, MA 
Public Record Request Number:2026-0099 
Requester: Avieana Rivera 
Request Date: Monday, March 2, 2026 4:37:00 PM 
Response Due Date: Monday, March 16, 2026 

Dear  Avieana Rivera : 

We have received your public Records request for Malden. This record is being assigned to appropriate department. The department reviewer will review the request and create an effort estimate to create the response. Per state mandate, for Malden, if the effort estimate is more than 2 hours, we will create a proposed cost estimate and will email such estimate to your attention. Once you approve the amount and send the payment, we will start working on your request. 

In case the estimate is less than 2 hours, we will proceed to work on the response and will send you the response once the work is completed. Please also note that state mandate allows us to complete and send the response within 10 business days. We will do our due diligence towards this effort. However, the due date may be extended for any of the following reasons: 
1.   Any clarification that is required from you towards this request will stop the clock. The clock will be resumed once the proper clarification is received. 
2.   Payment of estimated effort – The time it takes to receive the due amount will be added to the original due date. 
3.    If in the opinion of the reviewer it is determined that original due date cannot be met despite reasonable effort, the due date shall be extended based on reviewer’s judgment. You will be promptly notified in such case with revised due date. 
Thank you. 
Janell Summers, Assigned Department Records AdministratorSchool Department 

Malden 
110 Pleasant St 
Malden, MA 02148 
Tel: (781)-397-6100 
Email: jsummers@cityofmalden.org

communications.subject: Request# 2026-0099 : Response to your Request
communications.communication:
Please click here to download your response document(s). (https://www.townforms.com/FOIADirect-MaldenMACitizens/Public/Request/ResponseDocDownloadLanding.aspx?pRId=ZqKylrkyi7k%3d&pCompId=vksC0EAD%2bV0%3d) 
Please DO NOT reply to this email !! Please use the email address of the sender at bottom of this email, in order to communicate with the request management team. Malden, MA Public Record Request Number:2026-0099 
Requester: Avieana Rivera 
Request Date: Monday, March 2, 2026 4:37:00 PM 
Response Due Date: Monday, March 16, 2026 
Dear Avieana Rivera: 
We have completed the work in reference to your request as referenced above. The response is given below. Request Response Links to the School budgets for the years requested are available at: 
https://www.cityofmalden.org/271/City-Budget Attachments 
This response may or may not contain separate Response Documents to include specific response and data. In case such separate response documents exist, they are represented by attachments to this response email. Therefore, please look for any attachments if they exist. Between Request Response and attachments, we believe you are receiving a comprehensive response to your request. 
If you have any questions or comments, please do not hesitate to contact us at the following email address. 
Thank you. Janell Summers, Assigned Department Records AdministratorSchool Department 
Malden 
110 Pleasant St 
Malden, MA 02148 
Tel: (781)-397-6100 
Email: jsummers@cityofmalden.org 
You are advised that if you object to this response to your request, you have the right to petition the Commonwealth of Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. Your petition for appeal of this response must be made within 90 calendar days of the date of this response, in writing, and must specify the nature of your objections to this response, and include a copy of your electronic request with all header information including the time, date, subject, sender and recipient email address, and a copy of the within written response, and the attachment(s) related hereto. 
Your petition should be sent to the Supervisor at the following address: Supervisor of Records Division of Public Records, Office of the Secretary of the Commonwealth, One Ashburton Place, Room 1719, Boston, Massachusetts 02108 or: pre@sec.state.ma.us. Electronic communication is strongly encouraged and is the preferred method of correspondence.

communications.subject: WCTO FOIA request expense reports (MuckRock)
communications.communication:
Good morning.

Your FOIA Request has been received. The FOIA Department is closed to the public until further notice. The Wayne County Treasurer FOIA Department will notify you via e-mail when the FOIA Response is complete.  In addition, you will be sent an estimate of cost regarding your request.

MAIL IN AND DROP BOX PAYMENTS must be made in the form of a money order or cashier’s check (no personal checks or business checks accepted). Please include the invoice letter with all payments. Payments without an invoice letter may be returned. Mail in payments should be addressed to the Wayne County Treasurer, International Center Building, 400 Monroe, 5th Floor, Detroit, Michigan, 48226. Drop Box Payments can be placed in the drop box located on the 1st floor just outside of the FOIA Department. Please write FOIA Department on your (mail in/drop box) envelope. Once your payment is processed, your FOIA response will be emailed to you in 1-5 business days (excluding weekend and holidays).

PAYING BY CASH OR CREDIT CARD please make an appointment online at https://www.waynecountymi.gov/Government/Elected-Officials/Treasurer to visit the Cashier Department. In order to process your payment in person you must print out your invoice letter and bring it in along with your payment.  After making your payment please place a copy of your paid receipt along with your invoice letter in the drop box (envelopes are provided at the drop box) and the records will be emailed to you in 1-5 business days (excluding weekend and holidays). Please write FOIA Department on the envelope.

The Wayne County Treasurer’s Office policy is to process all FOIA requests in the order that they are received. The standard turnaround time frame for a response is between five (5) to fifteen (15) business days. If your response is not prepared in (5) business days you will receive an extension letter notifying you that additional time is needed to complete your request.

IF YOU HAVE NOT RECEIVED A RESPONSE WITHIN 5 BUSINESS DAYS (EXCLUDING WEEKEND AND HOLIDAY’S) PLEASE CHECK YOUR SPAM FOLDER.

Send all inquiries regarding FOIA to WCTFOIA@waynecountymi.gov


Thank you...”Stay Safe and we apologize for any inconvenience this may cause.”



Office of the Wayne County Treasurer
Eric R. Sabree
International Center Building
400 Monroe, 5th Floor
Detroit, Michigan 48226
WCTFOIA@waynecounty.com<mailto:WCTFOIA@waynecounty.com>
www.treasurer.waynecounty.com<http://www.treasurer.waynecounty.com/>

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Skagit County Sheriff (washington))

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Skagit County Sheriff (washington))
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

communications.subject: Your Skagit County, WA public records request #26-229 has been submitted.
communications.communication:
Skagit County, WA ************************************************************************ Your record request #26-229 has been submitted successfully. Thank you for submitting your request to Skagit County Records Department. Someone from our records team will respond as required by RCW 42.56.520, within five business days. ln calculating the five business days, the following are not counted: the day the county receives the request, Saturdays, Sundays and holidays. RCW 1.12.040. See also WAC 44-14-03006. lf the request is received by the County after County business hours. the request shall be deemed to have been received by the county on the next business day. A response may be (a) providing the records, (b) providing a link to records available online, (c) providing a reasonable estimated date of when the records will be available, (d) requesting clarification, or (e) denial of the request. If you have any questions or concerns, please contact me at 360-416-1152, or by email at pherman@co.skagit.wa.us. Sincerely, Pamela Herman Certified Public Records Officer, Central Services Skagit County 360-416-1152 Public Records Act (PRA) Notice. Any information, including certain personal information, submitted to the County by e-mail or other method may be subject to disclosure as a public record under RCW chapter 42.56.  ************************************************************************ Reply to this email or sign in to contact Skagit County, WA.

Chicago Public Schools (CPS) responds to all public records requests in accordance with the Illinois Freedom of Information Act (FOIA), 5 ILCS 140/1 et seq. 
Due to the volume and complexity of requests received in the normal course of business, CPS is extending the time for its initial response to your request by an additional 5 business days (to 10 business days total beginning on the first business day following receipt of the request) pursuant to 5 ILCS 140/3(e)(v) which allows for the examination and evaluation of the requested records by personnel to determine if they are exempt from disclosure under Section 7 of Illinois FOIA or should be revealed only with appropriate deletions. 
Upon the completion of a response, CPS will provide the first 50 pages to you at no charge.  If a response is more than 50 pages, a fee of 15¢ per page will be charged for each additional page. All applicable fees must be paid prior to the copying. CPS will inform you of any fees due if your paper response exceeds 50 pages. You can monitor the progress of your request at the link below and you’ll receive an email when your request has been completed. Thank you for using the CPS FOIA Center.

title: Request to Polk County Sheriff for Documents and Training on Dealing with Individuals with Autism

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Request to Polk County Sheriff for Documents and Training on Dealing with Individuals with Autism
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

I am requesting access to documents held by the Polk County Sheriff's Office related to their training and procedures for dealing with individuals with autism.

I am aware of the Polk County Sheriff's Office's Autism Decal program, which provides a way for individuals with autism and their caregivers to inform law enforcement officers of their condition. I am interested in any records, reports, memoranda, emails, or other documents related to the development and implementation of this program, including any policies, guidelines, or procedures related to the program.

Specifically, I am seeking any records, reports, memoranda, emails, or other documents related to the Polk County Sheriff's Office's training and procedures for dealing with individuals with autism, including any policies, guidelines, or procedures related to this training or procedures. I am also requesting any communications between the Polk County Sheriff's Office and other law enforcement agencies or government entities related to the training or procedures for dealing with individuals with autism.

Additionally, I am interested in any records, reports, memoranda, emails, or other documents related to any incidents involving individuals with autism and the Polk County Sheriff's Office, including any use of force or arrests.

I want to emphasize that I do not consent to the withholding, deletion, or redaction of any responsive records or documents, except in cases where the Polk County Sheriff's Office can provide a specific justification for why the records are exempt from disclosure under the Florida Public Records Law. Please provide me with any non-exempt records or documents in their entirety, including any segregable portions that may be released.

Furthermore, I hereby provide notice that any attempt to withhold, redact, or delete responsive records or documents without providing a specific justification for why they are exempt from disclosure will be met with a complaint and legal action to compel the Polk County Sheriff's Office to provide access to the requested records in accordance with the Florida Public Records Law.

In support of this request, I would like to reference relevant case law that affirms the public's right to access government records. One key case is Times Publishing Co. v. City of St. Petersburg, 558 So. 2d 487 (Fla. 2d DCA 1990), which held that the Florida Public Records Law reflects a "strong public policy in favor of access to public records." Another important case is News-Press Publishing Co. v. Carlson, 410 So. 2d 546 (Fla. 2d DCA 1982), which held that the Florida Public Records Law applies to all "writings made or received by an agency in connection with official business."

If any responsive records or documents are exempt from disclosure, I request that you provide me with a justification for each exemption relied upon.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Zackary DeCicco

communications.subject: FOIA Request
communications.communication:
This will acknowledge receipt of your FOIA request received February 6,
2026, wherein you are seeking information pursuant to the
Connecticut Freedom of Information Act.



Please be advised that the City of Danbury is firmly committed to providing
prompt access to all records subject to disclosure under Connecticut
General Statutes §1-210, et. seq.  Accordingly, all documents will be
assembled and reviewed as quickly as reasonably possible in light of other
responsibilities and immediate commitments.  Although the City of Danbury
takes your request seriously and will respond as promptly as possible, we
reserve the right to petition the Freedom of Information Commission for a
declaratory ruling regarding requests that may be exempt from disclosure.



Please note, that there will be a charge of $.50 per page, and any amount
of over $10.00 must be paid in advance.



If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact our office.

title: Arizona Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety audits and network sharing (Maricopa County Sheriff's Office)

communications.subject: Arizona Public Records Law Request: Arizona Public Records Act Request: Flock Safety audits and network sharing (Maricopa County Sheriff's Office)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Arizona Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Records related to the department’s Flock Safety system:

1. Organization Audit in Flock. 
The report should include data logged from the period of December 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Organization Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches done within the agency.

2. Network Audit in Flock.
The report should include data logged from the period of December 1, 2024, to the date this request is processed. Per Flock's documentation, the Network Audit is available within the Insights tab and is defined as searches of the organization’s Flock network by any agency in the Flock System.

3. A list of network share settings that include:
3a. Flock networks shared with me
3b. Flock networks shared with me Devices List (Please see https://cdn.muckrock.com/foia_files/2022/11/17/CPRA_REQUEST.pdf
https://cdn.muckrock.com/foia_files/2022/11/09/1938_001.pdf as examples of the lists from other jurisdictions.
3c. Networks that I’m sharing

Please provide records in their original electronic, machine readable format. For example, please provide csv or spreadsheet files where applicable. Please do not redirect me to your transparency portal.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Paul Tepper

communications.subject: FOIA Recieved 2160
communications.communication:
Greetings,

The Maricopa County Sheriff’s Office has received your request for public records and is processing your request. All requests are processed in the order in which they are received, and no requests are prioritized by deadline date. The amount of time to completely process your request depends upon the number of pending requests, volume of material encompassed in the request, and the amount of redaction, if any, that is necessary.

Please be aware, if you are requesting records with large amount of body camera videos and or high-profile cases, the time to complete your request may be significantly longer as these cases are often voluminous. Identifying the specific record(s) being requested may help expedite your request. You will be notified once records are made available for you to download or pick up, or if no records are found. Your tracking # is 2160. When checking the status of your request, please send emails to mediarequest@mcso.maricopa.gov<mailto:mediarequest@mcso.maricopa.gov> and include your tracking #.

***Pursuant to ARS 39-129, the Maricopa County Sheriff’s Office (MCSO) Legal Liaison Section has implemented a fee schedule for requests for video recordings.  The fee for processing video recordings is $25 per each video-hour ($6.25 per quarter hour).  In the event there are contemporaneous multiple requests for the same video, the cost associated with processing the video will be shared equally between all requesting parties at that time. Requests for previously processed videos will be at no cost unless the requestor wishes to receive a hard copy instead of an electronic copy. Fees for hardcopies are listed below. ***

The fee breakdown for hard copies are as follows:

Documents:                       $.50 for each page

CD’s:                                      $15.00 each

Thank you

—
Calbert Gillett
[Sent from Front]

Hello,

Your records are available and can be downloaded HERE<https://mcso.sharefile.com/d-s233a017ba6274dbda05e7b6a05d289a8> for free for the next ten (10) business days. Once you click on the link you will need to input your name and email address to access the records. The link will automatically expire in 10 business days and files are deleted. If you fail to download these files, you will need to resubmit a new request to obtain copies. If you would like a hard copy, please respond to this email and an appointment will be set up for purchasing.

Below is the process and fee associated with requesting hard copies.

Hard copies will be available only by appointments only. Monday through Friday from 9 a.m. to 4 p.m., excluding holidays.  Your records will be available for review or purchase for the next 30 business days from the date of this notice. A price will be provided if you choose to receive hard copies.

Thank you

—
Calbert Gillett
[Sent from Front]

communications.subject: FOIA Request 26-00043-F Final Response (ADB)
communications.communication:
ADB
Final Letter

[cid:image001.png@01DCAAF9.106D9D30]

U.S. Department of Justice
Civil Rights Division
Freedom of Information/PA Unit -4CON
950 Pennsylvania Ave., NW
Washington, DC 20530


Via Electronic Mail Only


Dear Mr. Steinbaugh,

This is in further response to your November 3, 2025  Freedom of Information Act/Privacy Act request seeking access to records pertaining to cases opened since January 1, 2025 through November 1, 2025, where the case description or subject matter involves the First Amendment.

After reviewing the responsive Civil Rights Division documents, I have determined that the attached document may be released to you in full.

If you are not satisfied with my response to this request, you may administratively appeal by writing to the Director, Office of Information Policy (OIP), United States Department of Justice, 441 G Street, NW, 6th Floor, Washington, DC 20530-0001, or you may submit an appeal through OIP's FOIAonline portal by creating an account on the following web site:  https://www.justice.gov/oip/submit-and-track-request-or-appeal.  Your appeal must be postmarked or electronically transmitted within 90 days of the date of my response to your request. If you submit your appeal by mail, both the letter and the envelope should be clearly marked "Freedom of Information Act Appeal." If possible, please provide a copy of your original request and this response letter with your appeal.

Additionally, you may contact the Office of Government Information Services (OGIS) at the National Archives and Records Administration to inquire about the FOIA medication services they offer. The contact information for OGIS is: Office of Government Information Services, National Archives and Records Administration, 8601 Adelphi Road - OGIS, College Park, Maryland 20740-6001; e-mail at ogis@nara.gov<mailto:ogis@nara.gov>; telephone at 202-741-5770; toll free at 1-877-684-6448; or facsimile at 202-741-5769. Please reference the FOIA/PA No. above in any correspondence with OGIS.

 I hope the Civil Rights Division has been of some assistance to you in this matter.

Sincerely,

Andrea D. Banks
Freedom of Information/Privacy Acts Unit
Civil Rights Division

communications.subject: Non-Commercial Police FOIA Request :: P138072-012126
communications.communication:
--- --- 
Dear requester, Thank you for your request for information pursuant tothe Illinois Freedom of Information Act.  Under the Freedom of Information Act, a public body may extend the time to respond to a FOIArequest by up to 5 business days for a limited number of reasons. 5 ILCS 140/3(e). We are extending thetime to respond to your request by 5 business days for the followingreason(s) : Before we can determine whether to provide the documents in response to your FOIArequest, we must consult with additional CPD units which have asubstantial interest in the determination of how to respond to this request and we cannot comply with the request for records within the 5 business day time-limit without unduly burdening or interfering with our operations. Your new due date if Feb 5, 2026. CPD

title: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Wicomico County Sheriff)

communications.subject: Maryland Public Information Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Wicomico County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Maryland Public Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

Use of force (UOF) data dating from January 1, 2017 to December 31, 2025. If possible, please provide this data in database or spreadsheet format and include the case number, incident date, incident time, subject injured indicator, subject injury force related info (e.g. Yes or No), subject age, subject gender, subject ethnicity, subject weapon type, incident area occurred, force type, officer division (e.g. law enforcement operations, detention/correctional operations, etc.), badge number and names associated with the officers involved. If one or more of these fields or dates is unavailable, provide all fields and/or information that is available responsive to my request. If no database or spreadsheet format is available, please provide copies of each individual use of force report or other report where use of force information is documented/reported by officers. Note, this is for all use of force incidents reported by your agency, not limited to only those incidents required to be reported under state or federal programs.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Samuel Sinyangwe

Mr. Sinyangwe,

This correspondence acknowledges receipt of your Maryland Public Information Act request submitted via MuckRock and received by the Wicomico County Sheriff’s Office on January 28, 2026, concerning use of force data for the period January 1, 2017, through December 31, 2025.

The Sheriff’s Office is currently unable to fully retrieve the requested data due to a software-related issue affecting the vendor-managed system from which the responsive records must be obtained. The vendor has been contacted and hopefully will have this resolved within thirty (30) days of the date your request was received.

Pursuant to § 4-203 of the Maryland General Provisions Article, the Sheriff’s Office is exercising its statutory authority to extend the response period where additional time is necessary to locate, retrieve, review, and prepare responsive records. This request remains under active review, and the delay is attributable to technical limitations beyond the immediate control of this office. This correspondence is not a denial of your request, nor a determination that responsive records do not exist.

Upon restoration of system access, the Sheriff’s Office will continue processing your request and will notify you of the availability of responsive records. You will also be advised in advance of any fees associated with the search, preparation, or reproduction of records, as required by law, before any release.

We appreciate your patience and understanding as we work to fulfill your request in compliance with the Maryland Public Information Act.

Sincerely,

1st Sgt. Anthony Glenn

communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Sinyanqwe,
I am writing in reference to your MPIA request to provide you with a status update. At this time, the system used to retrieve the requested information is experiencing technical issues, and we have been unable to access the records. We are actively working to resolve the issue and fulfill your request as soon as possible. If the information can be successfully retrieved, I am hopeful that we will be able to complete your request by the end of next week. I sincerely apologize for the delay and appreciate your patience and understanding. Unfortunately, the technical difficulties are beyond our control. Due to the delay, once the information is retrieved, all fees associated with researching and providing the records will be waived. You will not be obligated to pay any charges related to this request. Additionally, please note that Lt. Murphy no longer handles MPIA requests. Should you need to follow up or have any questions, feel free to contact me directly at tglenn@wicomicocounty.org<mailto:tglenn@wicomicocounty.org>.
I will promptly email you as soon as I am able to obtain the requested information.

communications.subject: FW: Maryland Public Information Act Request: Sheriff use of force data (2017-2025) (Wicomico County Sheriff)
communications.communication:
Dear Mr. Sinyanqwe,
This correspondence is in reference to your request submitted pursuant to the Maryland Public Information Act (MPIA), Title 4 of the General Provisions Article of the Annotated Code of Maryland.
Our office is currently awaiting an update to our records management system. While this technical issue has affected the formatting of the data retrieved, it has not prevented us from accessing responsive records. In order to comply with the MPIA’s requirement to produce records within a reasonable time, we are providing the responsive documents at this time. Please note that due to the current system limitations, certain incidents may appear more than once under the same case number. Once the system update is completed, entries will be consolidated and reflected only once per associated case number.
Pursuant to § 4-206 of the General Provisions Article, although fees are typically assessed for the search, preparation, and reproduction of records, all fees associated with fulfilling this request are hereby waived due to the delay.
If you believe any portion of your request has been denied, in whole or in part, you have the right to seek review pursuant to § 4-1B-04 and § 4-362 of the General Provisions Article.
Should you have any questions or require clarification regarding the enclosed records, please do not hesitate to contact me.

title: Waitlists for programing in MA prisons

communications.subject: Massachusetts Public Records Law Request: Waitlists for programing in MA prisons
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

Most recently available DOC data on the number of inmates on waitlists and number of inmates enrolled for Academic Education Programs, Vocational Education Programs, and Technology Education Programs within Massachusetts state prisons.

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as we believe this request is in the public interest, as suggested but not stipulated by the recommendations of the Massachusetts Supervisor of Public Records. The requested documents will be made available to the general public free of charge as part of the public information service at MuckRock.com, and is not made for commercial usage.

I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Luke Wise

title: Meiner emails: Facebook comment

communications.subject: Florida Sunshine Law Request: Meiner emails: Facebook comment
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Florida Sunshine Law, I hereby request the following records:

Any electronic communications received by Mayor Steven Meiner between 7 January 2025 and 20 January 2025 regarding Raquel Pacheco's Facebook comments and the police questioning of Pacheco over those comments. If the emails include any attachments, please include those attachments as well.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Matthew Petti

Thank you for submitting a request for records to City of Miami Beach - City Clerk's Office. 
  
 Your request reference number is Request Number: PRR-2026-218 and your security key is 198CCF30. 
  
 Please be advised that if the nature and/or volume of the public records requested to be inspected, examined or copied requires extensive use of information technology resources and/or extensive clerical or supervisory assistance by City personnel, you will be required to pay a service charge for the costs incurred for such extensive use of resources and/or assistance. The City will charge 15 cents for each copy provided, as well as for extensive labor costs (labor exceeding 30 minutes) involved in the responding to the records request. 
  
 Note that your Public Records Request has been assigned Request Number PRR-2026-218. 
  
 Please reference said request number# when communicating with the City of Miami Beach - City Clerk's Office regarding your request. If you have any questions, please contact us at (305.673.7411.

communications.subject: Public Records Request - PRR-2026-218 - Email Cost Estimate
communications.communication:
Good afternoon Matthew Petti, 
  
 The following is the email cost estimate for your request, Request Number: PRR-2026-218: 
  
 The cost estimate associated with your request includes a special service charge based on the labor cost of the employee providing the service of compiling the records and reviewing the records for any information that is confidential or exempt. Please note that the special service charge is for time spent in excess of thirty minutes. The cost estimate for the records in response to your request is as follows: 
  
 Email search resulted in 21 emails. 
  
 We charge the hourly rate of the lowest person in the department capable of handling the email review: 
  
 It took the I.T. Technician 30 minutes at a rate of $55.00 an hour = $0.00 
  
 We charge the hourly rate for every 100 pages reviewed at a rate of $40.00 for a total number of pages, 46 = $40.00 
  
 A 50% percent deposit is required prior to reviewing the records in the amount of $20.00. Upon receipt of payment, we will process the request. 
  
 Please respond by February 7, 2026, or this request will be considered closed. 
  
 Regards, 
  
 Ethan Lane
City of Miami Beach - City Clerk's Office 
 305.673.7411

communications.subject: RE: Florida Sunshine Law Request #PRR-2026-218

Sincerely,
Matthew
